steps.title: Extract points and slope from the graph
steps.content: The graphed line shows a negative slope. Reading from the grid, the line passes through $(-4, 10)$ and $(2, 4)$. Calculate the slope: $$m = \frac{4 - 10}{2 - (-4)} = \frac{-6}{6} = -1$$

steps.title: Write the equation of the graphed line
steps.content:
Use the slope $m = -1$ and point $(2, 4)$ to find the equation of $y = f(x) + 11$:
$$y - 4 = -1(x - 2)$$
$$y = -x + 6$$

steps.title: Isolate f(x) from the transformation
steps.content:
The graphed line satisfies $y = f(x) + 11$, and we found $y = -x + 6$. Therefore:
$$f(x) + 11 = -x + 6$$
$$f(x) = -x - 5$$
Rewrite as $f(x) = -5 - x = -5 - 1 \cdot x$.

steps.title: Match the form to an answer choice
steps.content: Compare $f(x) = -5 - 1 \cdot x$ with choice A: $f(x) = -d - cx$. Setting $d = 5$ and $c = 1$ (both positive), the forms match perfectly. That matches choice A — A is correct.
